revision.text: #redirect[[en:Otomi_Shrine]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''大富神社'''（おおとみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]（旧[[豊前国]]）四郎丸に所在する神社。 [[神社本庁]]の｢[[別表神社]]｣の一。 元[[県社]]。
周囲は水田が広がるのどかな田園地帯である。
本殿は嘉永５年の建造。本殿の軒の構造は独特の構造になっている。 軒は桔木（はねぎ）構造で、通常の一本の拮木で軒を支えるのでなく、桔木を二重に使って軒を支えおり、便宜的に「はかり桔構造」と名づけられているが、全国的に見ても建築学的に貴重な軒構造とされている。
== 御祭神 == *本殿 応神天皇・仲哀天皇・神功皇后 *東殿 田心姫命・湍津姫命・市杵島姫命（宗像神） *西殿 表筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命（八幡神）
== 歴史 == 現在は、大富神社と称するが、古来は宗像八幡社とも称され、大富神社神輿の古い鏡には大富ノ神とも宗像ノ神とも記されているという。
*社記伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]の時代にあって代々の勅願祈祷を修めたとある。 *宗像神社宝鏡記によると、[[白鳳]]元年（[[671]]）山田庄長横武某に御神託があり、新宮殿を建立し祭神を祀った。 *[[日本三大実録]]によると、[[貞観]]2年（[[860]]）豊前国大富神に従五位下を授くとある。
**なお、当地（山田庄）には平安末期頃宇佐神宮の弥勒寺（[[神宮寺]]）があり、宇佐神宮との関係が深い。
== 遺跡 == *勅使井 古来宇佐宮への勅使がこの地に宿泊する時には、この井戸の水を汲んで供した。 現在では神官が宇佐宮にこの井戸の水(御神水)を持参している。
== お祭り == *節分祭 2月3日 *春季神幸祭（[[八屋祇園]]）4月29日～5月1日 **[[豊前感応楽]]（県重要無形文化財）西暦偶数年の4月30～5月1日 （[[記録作成等の措置を講ずべき国の無形民俗文化財]]＝[[選択無形民俗文化財]]とれている。） *抜穂祭 9月
== アクセス == *[[日豊本線]][[豊前松江駅]]下車、徒歩30～40分。 *[[日豊本線]][[宇島駅]]下車。[[豊前市バス]]櫛狩屋行き（本数少ない、日祝運休）。に乗車、大富神社前下車。 *[[国道10号]]大村横矢橋信号を右折、1㎞近く走ると右手に大富神社の鬱蒼とした森が見えてくる。神社西側に無料駐車場がある。
== 所在地 == *〒828-0011 福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番 *宮司 清原直嗣氏
== 関連項目 == *[[宇佐神宮]] *[[宗像大社]] *[[道の駅豊前おこしかけ|豊前おこしかけ]]
== 参考文献 == *『豊前地方誌』渡邊春見著。葦書房。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.buzen.fukuoka.jp/ 豊前市公式ホームページ] *[http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/ 国指定文化財等データベース]
[[Category:福岡県の神社|おおとみしんしや]]

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=大富神社| 所在地=福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番| 祭神=八幡神| 社格=国史見在社・県社・別表神社| 別名=宗像八幡社| 例祭=[[4月29日]]～[[5月1日]]（[[八屋祇園]]）| 神事=豊前感応楽| 画像=}}
'''大富神社'''（おおとみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]（旧[[豊前国]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。
==祭神== 主祭神として[[八幡神]]を祀り、[[宗像三女神|宗像神]]・[[住吉三神|住吉神]]を配祀する。 * 本殿 - [[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]（八幡神） * 東殿 - [[田心姫命]]・[[湍津姫命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]（宗像神） * 西殿 - 表筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命（住吉神）
==歴史== 現在は、大富神社と称するが、古来は宗像八幡社とも称され、大富神社神輿の古い鏡には大富ノ神とも宗像ノ神とも記されているという。社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]の時代にあって代々の勅願祈祷を修めたとある。宗像神社宝鏡記によると、[[白鳳]]元年（[[671年]]）、山田庄長横武某に御神託があり、新宮殿を建立し祭神を祀った。[[延喜式神名帳]]の記載はないが、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）10月3日条には豊前国大富神に従五位下を授くとある（式外社）。
当地（山田庄）には平安末期頃、[[宇佐神宮]]の弥勒寺（[[神宮寺]]）があり、宇佐神宮との関係が深い。境内には、古来宇佐宮への勅使がこの地に宿泊する際に水を汲んで供した「勅使井」と呼ばれる井戸があり、現在でも神職が宇佐宮にこの井戸の水（御神水）を持参している。
== 境内 == 周囲は水田が広がるのどかな田園地帯である。
本殿は[[嘉永]]5年（[[1852年]]）の建造。本殿の軒は桔木（はねぎ）構造であるが、通常の一本の拮木で軒を支えるのでなく、桔木を二重に使って軒を支える独特の構造で、便宜的に「はかり桔構造」と名づけられ、全国的に見ても建築学的に貴重な軒構造とされている。
== 祭事 == * 節分祭 2月3日 * 春季神幸祭（[[八屋祇園]]） 4月29日～5月1日 ** [[豊前感応楽]] 西暦偶数年の4月30～5月1日 （国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、県重要無形文化財） * 抜穂祭 9月
== アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[日豊本線]][[豊前松江駅]]下車、徒歩30～40分。 * JR九州日豊本線[[宇島駅]]下車。[[豊前市バス]]櫛狩屋行き（本数少ない、日祝運休）に乗車、大富神社前下車。 * [[国道10号]]大村横矢橋信号を右折、1㎞近く走ると右手に大富神社の鬱蒼とした森が見えてくる。神社西側に無料駐車場がある。
== 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[宗像大社]] * [[道の駅豊前おこしかけ|豊前おこしかけ]]
== 参考文献 == * 『豊前地方誌』渡邊春見著。葦書房。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ 八屋祇園の公式ホームページ]

{{神社| 名称=大富神社| 所在地=福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番| 祭神=住吉大神
宗像大神
八幡大神| 社格=国史見在社・県社・別表神社| 別名=宗像八幡社| 例祭=[[4月29日]]～[[5月1日]]（[[八屋祇園]]）| 神事=豊前感応楽| 画像=}} '''大富神社'''（おおとみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]（旧[[豊前国]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 ==祭神== [[住吉三神|住吉大神]]・[[宗像三女神|宗像大神]]・[[八幡神|八幡大神]]を祀る。 * 本殿 - [[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]（八幡大神） * 東殿 - [[田心姫命]]・[[湍津姫命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]（宗像大神） * 西殿 - 表筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命（住吉大神） ==歴史== 現在は、大富神社と称するが、古来は宗像八幡社とも称され、大富神社神輿の古い鏡には大富ノ神とも宗像ノ神とも記されているという。はじめ、真早という者が宗像三女神の[[神託]]が下されたのに始まり、宗像神社宝鏡記によると[[白鳳]]元年（[[671年]]）、山田庄長横武某に住吉大神・八幡大神の神託が祀ったという。 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、この地で疫病が流行したが、国主が当社神主に祈祷させると速やかに治まったという。[[延喜式神名帳]]の記載はないが、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）10月3日条には豊前国大富神に従五位下を授くとある（式外社）。 当地（山田庄）には平安末期頃、[[宇佐神宮]]の弥勒寺（[[神宮寺]]）があり、宇佐神宮との関係が深い。境内には、古来宇佐宮への勅使がこの地に宿泊する際に水を汲んで供した「勅使井」と呼ばれる井戸があり、現在でも神職が宇佐宮にこの井戸の水（御神水）を持参している。 == 境内 == 周囲は水田が広がるのどかな田園地帯である。 本殿は[[嘉永]]5年（[[1852年]]）の建造。本殿の軒は桔木（はねぎ）構造であるが、通常の一本の拮木で軒を支えるのでなく、桔木を二重に使って軒を支える独特の構造で、便宜的に「はかり桔構造」と名づけられ、全国的に見ても建築学的に貴重な軒構造とされている。 == 祭事 == * 節分祭　2月3日 * 春季神幸祭（[[八屋祇園]]）　4月29日～5月1日 ** 豊前感応楽　西暦偶数年の4月30～5月1日　（国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、県重要無形文化財） -- 天平13年（[[741年]]）に始まり、戦国時代に一時中断したが、延宝5年（[[1677年]]）に再興した。元は6月末の[[大祓|夏越の大祓]]の神事として行われていたが、明治初年より4月末の神幸祭とともに行われるようになった。 * 抜穂祭　9月 == アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[日豊本線]][[豊前松江駅]]下車、徒歩30～40分。 * JR九州日豊本線[[宇島駅]]下車。[[豊前市バス]]櫛狩屋行き（本数少ない、日祝運休）に乗車、大富神社前下車。 * [[国道10号]]大村横矢橋信号を右折、1㎞近く走ると右手に大富神社の鬱蒼とした森が見えてくる。神社西側に無料駐車場がある。 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[宗像大社]] * [[道の駅豊前おこしかけ|豊前おこしかけ]] == 参考文献 == * 『豊前地方誌』渡邊春見著。葦書房。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ 八屋祇園の公式ホームページ] {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

{{神社| 名称=大富神社| 所在地=福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番| 祭神=住吉大神
宗像大神
八幡大神| 社格=国史見在社・県社・別表神社| 別名=宗像八幡社| 例祭=[[4月29日]]～[[5月1日]]（[[八屋祇園]]）| 神事=豊前感応楽| 画像=}} '''大富神社'''（おおとみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]（旧[[豊前国]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 ==祭神== [[住吉三神|住吉大神]]・[[宗像三女神|宗像大神]]・[[八幡神|八幡大神]]を祀る。 * 本殿 - [[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]（八幡大神） * 東殿 - [[田心姫命]]・[[湍津姫命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]（宗像大神） * 西殿 - 表筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命（住吉大神） ==歴史== 現在は、大富神社と称するが、古来は宗像八幡社とも称され、大富神社神輿の古い鏡には大富ノ神とも宗像ノ神とも記されているという。はじめ、真早という者が宗像三女神の[[神託]]が下されたのに始まり、宗像神社宝鏡記によると[[白鳳]]元年（[[671年]]）、山田庄長横武某に住吉大神・八幡大神の神託が祀ったという。 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、この地で疫病が流行したが、国主が当社神主に祈祷させると速やかに治まったという。[[延喜式神名帳]]の記載はないが、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）10月3日条には豊前国大富神に従五位下を授くとある（式外社）。 当地（山田庄）には平安末期頃、[[宇佐神宮]]の弥勒寺（[[神宮寺]]）があり、宇佐神宮との関係が深い。境内には、古来宇佐宮への勅使がこの地に宿泊する際に水を汲んで供した「勅使井」と呼ばれる井戸があり、現在でも神職が宇佐宮にこの井戸の水（御神水）を持参している。 == 境内 == 周囲は水田が広がるのどかな田園地帯である。 本殿は[[嘉永]]5年（[[1852年]]）の建造。本殿の軒は桔木（はねぎ）構造であるが、通常の一本の拮木で軒を支えるのでなく、桔木を二重に使って軒を支える独特の構造で、便宜的に「はかり桔構造」と名づけられ、全国的に見ても建築学的に貴重な軒構造とされている。 == 祭事 == * 節分祭　2月3日 * 春季神幸祭（[[八屋祇園]]）　4月29日～5月1日 ** 豊前感応楽　西暦偶数年の4月30～5月1日　（国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、県重要無形文化財） -- 天平13年（[[741年]]）に始まり、戦国時代に一時中断したが、延宝5年（[[1677年]]）に再興した。元は6月末の[[大祓|夏越の大祓]]の神事として行われていたが、明治初年より4月末の神幸祭とともに行われるようになった。 * 抜穂祭　9月 == アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[日豊本線]][[豊前松江駅]]下車、徒歩30～40分。 * JR九州日豊本線[[宇島駅]]下車。[[豊前市バス]]櫛狩屋行き（本数少ない、日祝運休）に乗車、大富神社前下車。 * [[国道10号]]大村横矢橋信号を右折、1㎞近く走ると右手に大富神社の鬱蒼とした森が見えてくる。神社西側に無料駐車場がある。 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[宗像大社]] * [[道の駅豊前おこしかけ|豊前おこしかけ]] == 参考文献 == * 『豊前地方誌』渡邊春見著。葦書房。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ 八屋祇園の公式ホームページ] {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:豊前市]]

{{神社| 名称=大富神社| 所在地=福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番| 祭神=住吉大神
宗像大神
八幡大神| 社格=国史見在社・県社・別表神社| 別名=宗像八幡社| 例祭=[[4月29日]]～[[5月1日]]（[[八屋祇園]]）| 神事=豊前感応楽| 画像=}} '''大富神社'''（おおとみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]（旧[[豊前国]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 ==祭神== [[住吉三神|住吉大神]]・[[宗像三女神|宗像大神]]・[[八幡神|八幡大神]]を祀る。 * 本殿 - [[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]（八幡大神） * 東殿 - [[田心姫命]]・[[湍津姫命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]（宗像大神） * 西殿 - 表筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命（住吉大神） ==歴史== 現在は、大富神社と称するが、古来は宗像八幡社とも称され、大富神社神輿の古い鏡には大富ノ神とも宗像ノ神とも記されているという。はじめ、真早という者が宗像三女神の[[神託]]が下されたのに始まり、宗像神社宝鏡記によると[[白鳳]]元年（[[671年]]）、山田庄長横武某に住吉大神・八幡大神の神託が祀ったという。 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、この地で疫病が流行したが、国主が当社神主に祈祷させると速やかに治まったという。[[延喜式神名帳]]の記載はないが、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）10月3日条には豊前国大富神に従五位下を授くとある（式外社）。 当地（山田庄）には平安末期頃、[[宇佐神宮]]の弥勒寺（[[神宮寺]]）があり、宇佐神宮との関係が深い。境内には、古来宇佐宮への勅使がこの地に宿泊する際に水を汲んで供した「勅使井」と呼ばれる井戸があり、現在でも神職が宇佐宮にこの井戸の水（御神水）を持参している。 == 境内 == 周囲は水田が広がるのどかな田園地帯である。 本殿は[[嘉永]]5年（[[1852年]]）の建造。本殿の軒は桔木（はねぎ）構造であるが、通常の一本の拮木で軒を支えるのでなく、桔木を二重に使って軒を支える独特の構造で、便宜的に「はかり桔構造」と名づけられ、全国的に見ても建築学的に貴重な軒構造とされている。 == 祭事 == * 節分祭　2月3日 * 春季神幸祭（[[八屋祇園]]）　4月29日～5月1日 ** 豊前感応楽　西暦偶数年の4月30～5月1日　（国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、県重要無形文化財） -- 天平13年（[[741年]]）に始まり、戦国時代に一時中断したが、延宝5年（[[1677年]]）に再興した。元は6月末の[[大祓|夏越の大祓]]の神事として行われていたが、明治初年より4月末の神幸祭とともに行われるようになった。 * 抜穂祭　9月 == アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[日豊本線]][[豊前松江駅]]下車、徒歩30～40分。 * JR九州日豊本線[[宇島駅]]下車。[[豊前市バス]]櫛狩屋行き（本数少ない、日祝運休）に乗車、大富神社前下車。 * [[国道10号]]大村横矢橋信号を右折、1km近く走ると右手に大富神社の鬱蒼とした森が見えてくる。神社西側に無料駐車場がある。 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[宗像大社]] * [[道の駅豊前おこしかけ|豊前おこしかけ]] == 参考文献 == * 『豊前地方誌』渡邊春見著。葦書房。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ 八屋祇園の公式ホームページ] {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:豊前市]]

{{神社 |名称=大富神社 |画像 = [[File:Ootomijinja.JPG|260px]] |所在地=福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番 |祭神=住吉大神
宗像大神
八幡大神 |社格=国史見在社・県社・別表神社 |別名=宗像八幡社 |例祭=[[4月29日]]～[[5月1日]]（[[八屋祇園]]） |神事=豊前感応楽 |}} '''大富神社'''（おおとみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]（旧[[豊前国]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 ==祭神== [[住吉三神|住吉大神]]・[[宗像三女神|宗像大神]]・[[八幡神|八幡大神]]を祀る。 * 本殿 - [[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]（八幡大神） * 東殿 - [[田心姫命]]・[[湍津姫命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]（宗像大神） * 西殿 - 表筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命（住吉大神） ==歴史== 現在は、大富神社と称するが、古来は宗像八幡社とも称され、大富神社神輿の古い鏡には大富ノ神とも宗像ノ神とも記されているという。はじめ、真早という者が宗像三女神の[[神託]]が下されたのに始まり、宗像神社宝鏡記によると[[白鳳]]元年（[[671年]]）、山田庄長横武某に住吉大神・八幡大神の神託が祀ったという。 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、この地で疫病が流行したが、国主が当社神主に祈祷させると速やかに治まったという。[[延喜式神名帳]]の記載はないが、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）10月3日条には豊前国大富神に従五位下を授くとある（式外社）。 当地（山田庄）には平安末期頃、[[宇佐神宮]]の弥勒寺（[[神宮寺]]）があり、宇佐神宮との関係が深い。境内には、古来宇佐宮への勅使がこの地に宿泊する際に水を汲んで供した「勅使井」と呼ばれる井戸があり、現在でも神職が宇佐宮にこの井戸の水（御神水）を持参している。 == 境内 == 周囲は水田が広がるのどかな田園地帯である。 本殿は[[嘉永]]5年（[[1852年]]）の建造。本殿の軒は桔木（はねぎ）構造であるが、通常の一本の拮木で軒を支えるのでなく、桔木を二重に使って軒を支える独特の構造で、便宜的に「はかり桔構造」と名づけられ、全国的に見ても建築学的に貴重な軒構造とされている。 == 祭事 == * 節分祭　2月3日 * 春季神幸祭（[[八屋祇園]]）　4月29日～5月1日 ** 豊前感応楽　西暦偶数年の4月30～5月1日　（国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、県重要無形文化財） -- 天平13年（[[741年]]）に始まり、戦国時代に一時中断したが、延宝5年（[[1677年]]）に再興した。元は6月末の[[大祓|夏越の大祓]]の神事として行われていたが、明治初年より4月末の神幸祭とともに行われるようになった。 * 抜穂祭　9月 == アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[日豊本線]][[豊前松江駅]]下車、徒歩30～40分。 * JR九州日豊本線[[宇島駅]]下車。[[豊前市バス]]櫛狩屋行き（本数少ない、日祝運休）に乗車、大富神社前下車。 * [[国道10号]]大村横矢橋信号を右折、1km近く走ると右手に大富神社の鬱蒼とした森が見えてくる。神社西側に無料駐車場がある。 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[宗像大社]] * [[道の駅豊前おこしかけ|豊前おこしかけ]] == 参考文献 == * 『豊前地方誌』渡邊春見著。葦書房。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ 八屋祇園の公式ホームページ] {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:豊前市]]

{{神社 |名称=大富神社 |画像 = [[File:Ootomijinja.JPG|260px]] |所在地=福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|37|05.5|N|131|06|56.6|E|region:JP-40_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神=住吉大神

{{神社 |名称=大富神社 |画像 = [[File:Ootomijinja.JPG|260px]] |所在地=福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番 |ISO=JP-40 |緯度度=33|緯度分=36|緯度秒=17.2 |経度度=131|経度分=06|経度秒=04.8 |ラベル位置=left |祭神=住吉大神
宗像大神
八幡大神 |社格=国史見在社・県社・別表神社 |別名=宗像八幡社 |例祭=[[4月29日]]～[[5月1日]]（[[八屋祇園]]） |神事=豊前感応楽 |地図=Japan Fukuoka |}} '''大富神社'''（おおとみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]（旧[[豊前国]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 ==祭神== [[住吉三神|住吉大神]]・[[宗像三女神|宗像大神]]・[[八幡神|八幡大神]]を祀る。 * 本殿 - [[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]（八幡大神） * 東殿 - [[田心姫命]]・[[湍津姫命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]（宗像大神） * 西殿 - 表筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命（住吉大神） ==歴史== 現在は、大富神社と称するが、古来は宗像八幡社とも称され、大富神社神輿の古い鏡には大富ノ神とも宗像ノ神とも記されているという。はじめ、真早という者が宗像三女神の[[神託]]が下されたのに始まり、宗像神社宝鏡記によると[[白鳳]]元年（[[671年]]）、山田庄長横武某に住吉大神・八幡大神の神託が祀ったという。 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、この地で疫病が流行したが、国主が当社神主に祈祷させると速やかに治まったという。[[延喜式神名帳]]の記載はないが、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）10月3日条には豊前国大富神に従五位下を授くとある（式外社）。 当地（山田庄）には平安末期頃、[[宇佐神宮]]の弥勒寺（[[神宮寺]]）があり、宇佐神宮との関係が深い。境内には、古来宇佐宮への勅使がこの地に宿泊する際に水を汲んで供した「勅使井」と呼ばれる井戸があり、現在でも神職が宇佐宮にこの井戸の水（御神水）を持参している。 == 境内 == 周囲は水田が広がるのどかな田園地帯である。 本殿は[[嘉永]]5年（[[1852年]]）の建造。本殿の軒は桔木（はねぎ）構造であるが、通常の一本の拮木で軒を支えるのでなく、桔木を二重に使って軒を支える独特の構造で、便宜的に「はかり桔構造」と名づけられ、全国的に見ても建築学的に貴重な軒構造とされている。 == 祭事 == * 節分祭　2月3日 * 春季神幸祭（[[八屋祇園]]）　4月29日～5月1日 ** 豊前感応楽　西暦偶数年の4月30～5月1日　（国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、県重要無形文化財） -- 天平13年（[[741年]]）に始まり、戦国時代に一時中断したが、延宝5年（[[1677年]]）に再興した。元は6月末の[[大祓|夏越の大祓]]の神事として行われていたが、明治初年より4月末の神幸祭とともに行われるようになった。 * 抜穂祭　9月 == アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[日豊本線]][[豊前松江駅]]下車、徒歩30～40分。 * JR九州日豊本線[[宇島駅]]下車。[[豊前市バス]]櫛狩屋行き（本数少ない、日祝運休）に乗車、大富神社前下車。 * [[国道10号]]大村横矢橋信号を右折、1km近く走ると右手に大富神社の鬱蒼とした森が見えてくる。神社西側に無料駐車場がある。 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[宗像大社]] * [[道の駅豊前おこしかけ|豊前おこしかけ]] == 参考文献 == * 『豊前地方誌』渡邊春見著。葦書房。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ 八屋祇園の公式ホームページ] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:豊前市]]

{{神社 |名称=大富神社 |画像 = [[File:Ootomijinja.JPG|260px]] |所在地=福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番 |ISO=JP-40 |緯度度=33|緯度分=36|緯度秒=17.2 |経度度=131|経度分=06|経度秒=04.8 |ラベル位置=left |祭神=住吉大神
宗像大神

{{神社 |名称=大富神社 |画像 = [[File:Ootomijinja.JPG|260px]] |所在地=福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番 |ISO=JP-40 |緯度度=33|緯度分=36|緯度秒=17.2 |経度度=131|経度分=06|経度秒=04.8 |ラベル位置=left |祭神=住吉大神
宗像大神
八幡大神 |社格=国史見在社・県社・別表神社 |別名=宗像八幡社 |例祭=[[4月29日]] - [[5月1日]]（[[八屋祇園]]） |神事=豊前感応楽 |地図=Japan Fukuoka |}} '''大富神社'''（おおとみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]（旧[[豊前国]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 ==祭神== [[住吉三神|住吉大神]]・[[宗像三女神|宗像大神]]・[[八幡神|八幡大神]]を祀る。 * 本殿 - [[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]（八幡大神） * 東殿 - [[田心姫命]]・[[湍津姫命]]・[[市杵島姫命]]（宗像大神） * 西殿 - 表筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命（住吉大神） ==歴史== 現在は、大富神社と称するが、古来は宗像八幡社とも称され、大富神社神輿の古い鏡には大富ノ神とも宗像ノ神とも記されているという。はじめ、真早という者が宗像三女神の[[神託]]が下されたのに始まり、宗像神社宝鏡記によると[[白鳳]]元年（[[671年]]）、山田庄長横武某に住吉大神・八幡大神の神託が祀ったという。 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、この地で疫病が流行したが、国主が当社神主に祈祷させると速やかに治まったという。[[延喜式神名帳]]の記載はないが、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）10月3日条には豊前国大富神に従五位下を授くとある（式外社）。 当地（山田庄）には平安末期頃、[[宇佐神宮]]の弥勒寺（[[神宮寺]]）があり、宇佐神宮との関係が深い。境内には、古来宇佐宮への勅使がこの地に宿泊する際に水を汲んで供した「勅使井」と呼ばれる井戸があり、現在でも神職が宇佐宮にこの井戸の水（御神水）を持参している。 == 境内 == 周囲は水田が広がるのどかな田園地帯である。 本殿は[[嘉永]]5年（[[1852年]]）の建造。本殿の軒は桔木（はねぎ）構造であるが、通常の一本の拮木で軒を支えるのでなく、桔木を二重に使って軒を支える独特の構造で、便宜的に「はかり桔構造」と名づけられ、全国的に見ても建築学的に貴重な軒構造とされている。 == 祭事 == * 節分祭　2月3日 * 春季神幸祭（[[八屋祇園]]）　4月29日 - 5月1日 ** 豊前感応楽　西暦偶数年の4月30 - 5月1日　（国の[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、県重要無形文化財） -- 天平13年（[[741年]]）に始まり、戦国時代に一時中断したが、延宝5年（[[1677年]]）に再興した。元は6月末の[[大祓|夏越の大祓]]の神事として行われていたが、明治初年より4月末の神幸祭とともに行われるようになった。 * 抜穂祭　9月 == アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[日豊本線]] [[豊前松江駅]]下車、徒歩30 - 40分。 * JR日豊本線 [[宇島駅]]下車。[[豊前市バス]]櫛狩屋行き（本数少ない、日祝運休）に乗車、大富神社前下車。 * [[国道10号]]大村横矢橋信号を右折、1km近く走ると右手に大富神社の鬱蒼とした森が見えてくる。神社西側に無料駐車場がある。 == 参考文献 == * 『豊前地方誌』渡邊春見著。葦書房。 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[宗像大社]] * [[道の駅豊前おこしかけ|豊前おこしかけ]] ==外部リンク== *{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=八屋祇園の公式ホームページ |date=20190330122549}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:豊前市の歴史]] [[Category:豊前市の建築物]]

宗像大神

{{神社 |名称=大富神社 |画像 = [[File:Ootomijinja.JPG|260px]] |所在地=福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番 |ISO=JP-40 |緯度度=33|緯度分=36|緯度秒=17.2 |経度度=131|経度分=06|経度秒=04.8 |ラベル位置=left |祭神=宗像大神
八幡大神
住吉大神
斎主神 |社格=国史見在社・県社・別表神社 |別名=宗像八幡社 |例祭=[[4月3日]] |神事=豊前感応楽 |地図=Japan Fukuoka |}} '''大富神社'''（おおとみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]（旧[[豊前国]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 ==祭神== [[住吉三神|住吉大神]]・[[宗像三女神|宗像大神]]・[[八幡神|八幡大神]]・斎主神の10柱を祀る。 * 宗像大神：[[田心姫命]]・[[湍津姫命]]・[[市杵島姫命]] * 八幡大神：[[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]] * 住吉大神：表筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命 * 斎主神 ==歴史== 現在は、大富神社と称するが、古来は宗像八幡社とも称され、大富神社神輿の古い鏡には大富ノ神とも宗像ノ神とも記されているという。はじめ、真早という者が宗像三女神の[[神託]]が下されたのに始まり、宗像神社宝鏡記によると[[白鳳]]元年（[[671年]]）、山田庄長横武某に住吉大神・八幡大神の神託が祀ったという。 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、この地で疫病が流行したが、国主が当社神主に祈祷させると速やかに治まったという。[[延喜式神名帳]]の記載はないが、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）10月3日条には豊前国大富神に従五位下を授くとある（式外社）。 当地（山田庄）には平安末期頃、[[宇佐神宮]]の弥勒寺（[[神宮寺]]）があり、宇佐神宮との関係が深い。境内には、古来宇佐宮への勅使がこの地に宿泊する際に水を汲んで供した「勅使井」と呼ばれる井戸があり、現在でも神職が宇佐宮にこの井戸の水（御神水）を持参している。 == 境内 == 周囲は水田が広がるのどかな田園地帯である。 本殿は[[嘉永]]5年（[[1852年]]）の建造。本殿の軒は桔木（はねぎ）構造であるが、通常の一本の拮木で軒を支えるのでなく、桔木を二重に使って軒を支える独特の構造で、便宜的に「はかり桔構造」と名づけられ、全国的に見ても建築学的に貴重な軒構造とされている。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭　1月1日 * 節分祭　2月節分の日 * 建国祭　2月11日 * 祈年祭　2月17日 * 例祭　4月3日 * 古式春季神幸大祭（[[八屋祇園]]）　4月30日 - 5月1日（県指定重要無形文化財） ** 感応楽　[[重要無形民俗文化財|（国指定重要無形民俗文化財]]、ユネスコ無形文化遺産） -- 第42代[[文武天皇]]酉年（[[697年]]）に始まったとされる感応楽は、神幸祭に隔年（西暦の偶数年）に奉納される。 * 夏越祭　7月31日-8月1日 * 古式秋季御供揃祭　10月19日 * 新嘗祭　11月23日 * 除夜祭　12月31日 == アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[日豊本線]] [[豊前松江駅]]下車、徒歩30 - 40分。 * JR日豊本線 [[宇島駅]]下車。[[豊前市バス]]櫛狩屋行き（本数少ない、日祝運休）に乗車、大富神社前下車。 * [[国道10号]]大村横矢橋信号を右折、1km近く走ると右手に大富神社の鬱蒼とした森が見えてくる。神社西側に無料駐車場がある。 == 参考文献 == * 『豊前地方誌』渡邊春見著。葦書房。 == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[宗像大社]] * [[道の駅豊前おこしかけ|豊前おこしかけ]] ==外部リンク== ・[https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ 大富神社公式サイト] ・{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=八屋祇園の公式ホームページ |date=20190330122549}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:豊前市の歴史]] [[Category:豊前市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称=大富神社 |画像 = [[File:Ootomijinja.JPG|260px]] |所在地=福岡県豊前市大字四郎丸山田256番 |ISO=JP-40 |緯度度=33|緯度分=36|緯度秒=17.2 |経度度=131|経度分=06|経度秒=04.8 |ラベル位置=left |祭神=宗像大神
八幡大神
住吉大神

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name= Ōtomi Shrine |Image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |Location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |Deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |Annual Festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |Map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命}}, {{ill|Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命}}, {{ill|Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == Festivals == * New Year's Festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun Festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer Festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * Annual Festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring Festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer Festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn Festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest Festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End Festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Michi no Eki Buzen Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historical Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Special Shrines]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines]]

{{Shrine |Name= Ōtomi Shrine |Image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |Location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |Deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |Annual Festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |Map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命}}, {{ill|Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命}}, {{ill|Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == Festivals == * New Year's Festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun Festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer Festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * Annual Festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring Festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer Festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn Festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest Festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End Festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Michi no Eki Buzen Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historical Shrines]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Special Shrines]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]]

[[八幡大神]]
[[住吉大神]]
[[斎主神]] |社格=国史見在社・旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] |別名=宗像八幡社 |例祭=[[4月3日]] |神事=豊前感応楽 |地図={{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=大富神社|coordinates= {{coord2|33|36|17.2|N|131|6|4.8|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|36|17.2|N|131|6|4.8|E}}}} }} '''大富神社'''（おおとみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]（旧[[豊前国]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 ==祭神== [[住吉三神|住吉大神]]・[[宗像三女神|宗像大神]]・[[八幡神|八幡大神]]・斎主神の10柱を祀る。 * 宗像大神：[[田心姫命]]・[[湍津姫命]]・[[市杵島姫命]] * 八幡大神：[[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]] * 住吉大神：表筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命 * 斎主神 ==歴史== 現在は、大富神社と称するが、古来は宗像八幡社とも称され、大富神社神輿の古い鏡には大富ノ神とも宗像ノ神とも記されているという。はじめ、真早という者が宗像三女神の[[神託]]が下されたのに始まり、宗像神社宝鏡記によると[[白鳳]]元年（[[671年]]）、山田庄長横武某に住吉大神・八幡大神の神託が祀ったという。 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、この地で疫病が流行したが、国主が当社神主に祈祷させると速やかに治まったという。[[延喜式神名帳]]の記載はないが、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）10月3日条には豊前国大富神に従五位下を授くとある（式外社）。 当地（山田庄）には平安末期頃、[[宇佐神宮]]の弥勒寺（[[神宮寺]]）があり、宇佐神宮との関係が深い。境内には、古来宇佐宮への勅使がこの地に宿泊する際に水を汲んで供した「勅使井」と呼ばれる井戸があり、現在でも神職が宇佐宮にこの井戸の水（御神水）を持参している。 == 境内 == 周囲は水田が広がるのどかな田園地帯である。 本殿は[[嘉永]]5年（[[1852年]]）の建造。本殿の軒は桔木（はねぎ）構造であるが、通常の一本の拮木で軒を支えるのでなく、桔木を二重に使って軒を支える独特の構造で、便宜的に「はかり桔構造」と名づけられ、全国的に見ても建築学的に貴重な軒構造とされている。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭　1月1日 * 節分祭　2月節分の日 * 建国祭　2月11日 * 祈年祭　2月17日 * 例祭　4月3日 * 古式春季神幸大祭（[[八屋祇園]]）　4月30日 - 5月1日（県指定重要無形文化財） ** 感応楽　[[重要無形民俗文化財|（国指定重要無形民俗文化財]]、ユネスコ無形文化遺産） -- 第42代[[文武天皇]]酉年（[[697年]]）に始まったとされる感応楽は、神幸祭にて隔年（西暦の偶数年）で奉納される。 * 夏越祭　7月31日-8月1日 * 古式秋季御供揃祭　10月19日 * 新嘗祭　11月23日 * 除夜祭　12月31日 == アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[日豊本線]] [[豊前松江駅]]下車、徒歩30 - 40分。 * JR日豊本線 [[宇島駅]]下車。[[豊前市バス]]櫛狩屋行き（本数少ない、日祝運休）に乗車、大富神社前下車。 * [[国道10号]]大村横矢橋信号を右折、1km近く走ると右手に大富神社の鬱蒼とした森が見えてくる。神社西側に無料駐車場がある。 == 参考文献 == * 『豊前地方誌』渡邊春見著。葦書房。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[宗像大社]] * [[道の駅豊前おこしかけ|豊前おこしかけ]] ==外部リンク== ・[https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ 大富神社公式サイト] ・{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=八屋祇園の公式ホームページ |date=20190330122549}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:豊前市の歴史]] [[Category:豊前市の建築物]]

{{Shrine |Name= Ōtomi Shrine |Image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |Location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |Deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |Annual Festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |Map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命}}, {{ill|Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命}}, {{ill|Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == Festivals == * New Year's Festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun Festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer Festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * Annual Festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring Festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer Festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn Festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest Festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End Festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Michi no Eki Buzen Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historical Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]]

It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer Festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn Festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest Festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End Festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Michi no Eki Buzen Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historical Shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]]

Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. {{Shrine |Name= Ōtomi Shrine |Image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |Location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |Deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |Annual Festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |Map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命}}, {{ill|Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命}}, {{ill|Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == Festivals == * New Year's Festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun Festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day Festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer Festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * Annual Festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring Festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer Festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn Festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest Festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End Festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Michi no Eki Buzen Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] ]]

Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |annual festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命}}, {{ill|Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命}}, {{ill|Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * annual festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Michi no Eki Buzen Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] ]]

Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completed articles]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] ]]

The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completed articles]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORTOtomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] ]]

Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completed articles]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] ]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT::Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completed articles]] [[Category:defautsort]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] ]]

Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[Category:Completed articles]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] ]]

The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completed articles]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] ]]
