{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] ]]

Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}

Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]]

Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] [[Category:fix the O]]{{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[category:fix the O]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]]

Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]]

Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:goodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]]
