Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|12=draft}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Otomi Shrine]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]]

While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Otomi Shrine]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]]

Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] }} [[en:Otomi Shrine]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|12=draft}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] }} [[en:Otomi Shrine]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]]

Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former Rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}}[[Category:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]]

The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}} [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former Rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|qq=draft}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{citation needed|Ōtomi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: {{citation needed|Otomi Shrine}} [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]]

Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former Rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buzen City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]]

According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] * [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category: [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

The shrine's former Rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}} * Hachiman Ōkami: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] * [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former Rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}} * Hachiman Ōkami: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] * [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]]

revision.comment: 25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大富神社 up to rev 102019373
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=大富神社 |画像 = [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]大字四郎丸山田256番 |位置 = {{Coord|33|36|17.2|N|131|6|4.8|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[宗像大神]]
[[八幡大神]]
[[住吉大神]]
[[斎主神]] |社格=国史見在社・旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] |別名=宗像八幡社 |例祭=[[4月3日]] |神事=豊前感応楽 |地図={{Location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=大富神社|coordinates= {{coord2|33|36|17.2|N|131|6|4.8|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|36|17.2|N|131|6|4.8|E}}}} }} '''大富神社'''（おおとみじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[豊前市]]（旧[[豊前国]]）にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]で、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 ==祭神== [[住吉三神|住吉大神]]・[[宗像三女神|宗像大神]]・[[八幡神|八幡大神]]・斎主神の10柱を祀る。 * 宗像大神：[[田心姫命]]・[[湍津姫命]]・[[市杵島姫命]] * 八幡大神：[[応神天皇]]・[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]] * 住吉大神：表筒男命・中筒男命・底筒男命 * 斎主神 ==歴史== 現在は、大富神社と称するが、古来は宗像八幡社とも称され、大富神社神輿の古い鏡には大富ノ神とも宗像ノ神とも記されているという。はじめ、真早という者が宗像三女神の[[神託]]が下されたのに始まり、宗像神社宝鏡記によると[[白鳳]]元年（[[671年]]）、山田庄長横武某に住吉大神・八幡大神の神託が祀ったという。 社伝によると、[[崇神天皇]]の時代、この地で疫病が流行したが、国主が当社神主に祈祷させると速やかに治まったという。[[延喜式神名帳]]の記載はないが、[[日本三代実録]]の[[貞観_(日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）10月3日条には豊前国大富神に従五位下を授くとある（式外社）。 当地（山田庄）には平安末期頃、[[宇佐神宮]]の弥勒寺（[[神宮寺]]）があり、宇佐神宮との関係が深い。境内には、古来宇佐宮への勅使がこの地に宿泊する際に水を汲んで供した「勅使井」と呼ばれる井戸があり、現在でも神職が宇佐宮にこの井戸の水（御神水）を持参している。 == 境内 == 周囲は水田が広がるのどかな田園地帯である。 本殿は[[嘉永]]5年（[[1852年]]）の建造。本殿の軒は桔木（はねぎ）構造であるが、通常の一本の拮木で軒を支えるのでなく、桔木を二重に使って軒を支える独特の構造で、便宜的に「はかり桔構造」と名づけられ、全国的に見ても建築学的に貴重な軒構造とされている。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭　1月1日 * 節分祭　2月節分の日 * 建国祭　2月11日 * 祈年祭　2月17日 * 例祭　4月3日 * 古式春季神幸大祭（[[八屋祇園]]）　4月30日 - 5月1日（県指定重要無形文化財） ** 感応楽　[[重要無形民俗文化財|（国指定重要無形民俗文化財]]、ユネスコ無形文化遺産） -- 第42代[[文武天皇]]酉年（[[697年]]）に始まったとされる感応楽は、神幸祭にて隔年（西暦の偶数年）で奉納される。 * 夏越祭　7月31日-8月1日 * 古式秋季御供揃祭　10月19日 * 新嘗祭　11月23日 * 除夜祭　12月31日 == アクセス == * [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[日豊本線]] [[豊前松江駅]]下車、徒歩30 - 40分。 * JR日豊本線 [[宇島駅]]下車。[[豊前市バス]]櫛狩屋行き（本数少ない、日祝運休）に乗車、大富神社前下車。 * [[国道10号]]大村横矢橋信号を右折、1km近く走ると右手に大富神社の鬱蒼とした森が見えてくる。神社西側に無料駐車場がある。 == 参考文献 == * 『豊前地方誌』渡邊春見著。葦書房。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[宇佐神宮]] * [[宗像大社]] * [[道の駅豊前おこしかけ|豊前おこしかけ]] ==外部リンク== ・[https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ 大富神社公式サイト] ・{{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=八屋祇園の公式ホームページ |date=20190330122549}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとみしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:豊前市の歴史]] [[Category:豊前市の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former Rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}} * Hachiman Ōkami: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] * [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:大富神社]] {{translated page|ja|大富神社|version=102019373|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] * [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:大富神社]] {{translated page|ja|大富神社|version=102019373|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= [[File:Ootomi jinja.jpg|260px]] |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former Rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}} * Hachiman Ōkami: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] * [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:大富神社]] {{translated page|ja|大富神社|version=102019373|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:豊前市の建築物]]

While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]] * [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category: [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[sn:Otomi Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:大富神社]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|大富神社|version=102019373|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:豊前市の建築物]] [[Category:豊前市の歴史]]
