Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka |prefecture= {{ill|Buzen|WD=Q633601|1=Buzen, Fukuoka}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.604778|N|131.101333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former Rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū|1=Usa Jingū}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū|1=Usa Jingū}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|WD=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[ja:大富神社]] {{translated page|ja|大富神社|version=102019373|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11434509}} [[Category: [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Buzen City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Buzen Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Ōtomi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:大富神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Ōtomi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:大富神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Ōtomi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:大富神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ōtomi Shrine |image= File:Ootomi jinja.jpg |location= 256 Yamada, Shiromaru, Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture |ISO= JP-40 |LatDeg= 33 | LatMin= 36 | LatSec= 17.2 |LonDeg= 131 | LonMin= 06 | LonSec= 04.8 |Label Position= left |deity= Munakata Ōkami
Hachiman Ōkami
Sumiyoshi Ōkami
Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka |prefecture= {{ill|Buzen|WD=Q633601|1=Buzen, Fukuoka}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.604778|N|131.101333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former Rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū|1=Usa Jingū}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū|1=Usa Jingū}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|WD=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[ja:大富神社]] {{translated page|ja|大富神社|version=102019373|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11434509}} [[Category: [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Buzen City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Buzen Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

Sai no Kami |Rank= National Historical Shrine, Prefectural Shrine, Special Shrine |Alias= Munakata Hachiman Shrine |festival= April 3 |Rituals= Buzen Kanogaku |map= Japan Fukuoka |prefecture= {{ill|Buzen|WD=Q633601|1=Buzen, Fukuoka}} |coordinates= {{Coord|33.604778|N|131.101333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Ōtomi Shrine''' (大富神社, Ōtomi Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in Buzen City, Fukuoka Prefecture, formerly known as Buzen Province. The shrine's former Rank was Prefectural Shrine, and it is now listed as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == The shrine enshrines ten deities, including: * Munakata Ōkami: {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * Hachiman Ōkami: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} * Sumiyoshi Ōkami: {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokototsutsu no O no Mikoto|ja|底筒男命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Nakatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|中筒男命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatsutsuno no O no Mikoto|ja|表筒男命|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神}} * Sai no Kami == History == Though it is currently known as Ōtomi Shrine, it was formerly called Munakata Hachiman Shrine. The shrine's old mirrors refer to it as both Ōtomi no Kami and Munakata no Kami. It is said to have originated when a person named Mahaya received an oracle from the Munakata deities. According to the ''Munakata Shrine Hōkyōki'', in Hakuhō 1 (671), Chōyokubu of the Yamada estate enshrined the Sumiyoshi and Hachiman deities following an oracle. According to legend, during the reign of Emperor Sujin, an epidemic plagued the region, but the local lord had the shrine's priest pray for relief, which quickly followed. While it is not mentioned in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'', the ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'' records that on October 3, Jōgan 2 (860), the deity of Ōtomi Shrine in Buzen Province was awarded the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (shikigai-sha). The Yamada estate (now Buzen City) had close ties with {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū|1=Usa Jingū}} during the late Heian period. The shrine grounds contain a well called "Chokushi-i" (imperial envoy well), from which water was drawn for offerings when imperial envoys visited Usa Jingū. To this day, shrine priests still bring water from this well to Usa Jingū. == Grounds == The shrine is located in a tranquil rural area surrounded by rice fields. The main shrine building dates back to Kaei 5 (1852). The building features a unique "hakarigi" structure, where the eaves are supported by double horizontal beams, making it a rare and architecturally significant design in Japan. == festivals == * New Year's festival (Saitansai) – January 1 * Setsubun festival – Setsubun Day in February * National Foundation Day festival (Kenkokusai) – February 11 * Spring Prayer festival (Kinensai) – February 17 * festival (Reisai) – April 3 * Ancient Spring festival (Hachiya Gion) – April 30 to May 1 (designated as an Important Intangible Cultural Property of Fukuoka Prefecture) * Kanogaku – A ritual dance designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan and a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, believed to have started in the year of Emperor Monmu's reign in 697. It is performed every other year (even-numbered years) during the shrine's sacred festival. * Summer festival (Nagoshi-sai) – July 31 to August 1 * Ancient Autumn festival (Okusai) – October 19 * Harvest festival (Niinamesai) – November 23 * Year-End festival (Joyasai) – December 31 == Access == * By Train: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Buzen Matsue Station, walk 30-40 minutes. * By Bus: JR Nippō Main Line, get off at Usa Station. Take the Buzen City Bus bound for Kushikariya (limited service, no service on Sundays and holidays), get off at Ōtomi Shrine-mae. * By Car: From National Route 10, turn right at the Omura Yokoya Bridge signal, and after about 1 km, the shrine's dense forest will be visible on the right. Free parking is available on the west side of the shrine. == References == * Harumi Watanabe, ''History of the Buzen Region''. Ashishobo Publishing. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū|1=Usa Jingū}} * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|WD=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}} * {{ill|Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|ja|道の駅豊前おこしかけ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Roadside station Buzen-Okoshikake|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://ootomijinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Ōtomi Shrine] * {{Wayback|url=http://www.geocities.jp/hatiyagion/ |title=Hachiya Gion Official Website |date=20190330122549}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ }} {{draft categories| [[ja:大富神社]] {{translated page|ja|大富神社|version=102019373|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11434509}} [[Category: [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Buzen City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Buzen Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
